text: Today we are talking about fine tuning language models without retraining every parameter.
A common approach is LoRA, which stands for Low-Rank Adaptation, and it inserts small trainable matrices into the model.
The optimizer still uses backpropagation, but the memory footprint is much smaller.
Later, the team compares retrieval augmented generation with standard supervised fine tuning.
They also discuss quantization and how lower precision weights can make inference cheaper.
